question.text: Prakash, aged 55, is planning to retire at 60. He has Rs.30 lakh in outstanding loans. What should be his debt management approach?

answer.text: Focus entirely on retirement saving and ignore the loans
Create a prioritized repayment plan to become debt-free before retirement
Take additional loans to pay off existing ones at lower rates
Extend all loan tenures beyond age 70

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Creating a prioritized repayment plan — paying off high-cost debt first and systematically reducing all obligations before retirement — ensures the retiree starts with zero mandatory debt payments. **The Trap:** Option C (debt consolidation) may help in some cases but the goal should be elimination, not just restructuring. Option D pushes obligations deep into retirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Pre-retirement debt management is a key advisory area in Series XVII and connects to financial health assessment in Series X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rank debts by interest rate: credit cards (18-36%) → personal loans (12-18%) → auto loans (8-12%) → home loan (7-9%). Attack highest cost first while maintaining minimums on others.
